mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in payroll growth above 150k jobs per month signals tightening labor market conditions that pressure corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k, or the signal's IC drops below -0.05 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and consumer confidence indicate weak demand fundamentals, creating a negative feedback loop that drives S&P 500 forward returns lower over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns are positive in > 50% of cases where both HOUST and CPIAUCSL decline, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production above its 12-month moving average signals expanding supply-side capacity, which reduces inflationary pressures and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 40% of instances where INDPRO exceeds its 12-month MA, or the signal's IC falls below 0.02 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A negative real interest rate (FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL) below 1% indicates accommodative financial conditions that boost equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL < 1%, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in unemployment rate and rise in real compensation (RSAFS) signals strong labor market dynamics that support corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 40% of cases where UNRATE falls and RSAFS rises, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDP growth below 2% annualized combined with rising housing starts indicates conflicting demand signals, creating uncertainty that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 50% of instances where GDPC1 < 2% and HOUST > 600, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production above 1% month-over-month combined with stable inflation (CPIAUCSL < 2% YoY) indicates favorable supply-side conditions that drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 40% of instances where INDPRO > 1% MoM and CPIAUCSL < 2% YoY, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
